title: Computer science enrollment fell for the first time in 20 years after ChatGPT's rise, a Stanford economist says
excerpt: A Stanford economist says AI may be reshaping students' choices as computer science enrollment slows.
content_html:
By Thibault Spirlet

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

The explosive rise of ChatGPT may coincide with a turning point for one of America's fastest-growing college subjects.

Computer science enrollment declined during the 2025-26 academic year for the first time in roughly two decades, according to new research by Jacob Light, an economist at Stanford University's Hoover Institution.

While the study doesn't estimate the size of the drop, separate National Student Clearinghouse Research Center data showed undergraduate enrollment in Computer and Information Sciences and Support Services at four-year colleges fell 8.1% year over year in the fall of 2025.

The timing overlaps with the rapid adoption of generative AI, but Light cautioned that the data does not show artificial intelligence caused the decline.

Using a dataset covering more than 50 million course sections offered since 1996 across 1,019 US colleges and universities, Light tracked long-term enrollment patterns alongside the growing capabilities of large language models.

Light told Business Insider that, at the average school in his sample, enrollment in computer science classes fell about 4.6% year over year, while computer science's share of overall course enrollment dropped 6.3 percentage points.

"The most notable recent development is the flattening and reversal in the Computer Science enrollment share trend," Light wrote. "In 2025-26, Computer Science enrollment share declined for the first time in roughly two decades, interrupting a period of sustained and unusually rapid growth."

'Descriptive' rather than 'causal' effect of AI

Computer science had enjoyed years of booming popularity as demand for software engineers and other tech workers surged.

The number of bachelor's degrees awarded in computer and information sciences more than doubled over the past decade, rising from about 56,000 in 2014 to about 122,000 in 2024, according to data from the National Science Foundation's National Center for Science and Engineering Statistics.

But the arrival of AI assistants capable of writing code, debugging programs, and completing technical assignments has sparked debate about whether the field's value proposition is changing.

Light told Business Insider there are five leading explanations for the enrollment drop: a weaker entry-level software engineering job market, uncertainty about the value of computer science degrees in the AI era, weaker academic preparation among incoming students, changes in college demographics linked to immigration policy, and the expansion of competing fields such as data science.

He added that the data cannot distinguish among those possibilities.

"Therefore, I treat the enrollment patterns as descriptive evidence rather than as an estimate of the causal effect of ChatGPT or AI exposure on student demand," Light wrote.

The finding is also consistent with data from the National Student Clearinghouse Research Center.

Its Fall 2025 enrollment report found that undergraduate enrollment in Computer and Information Sciences and Support Services at four-year colleges fell 8.1% year over year, from about 659,700 students in 2024 to 606,100 in 2025.

Even so, enrollment remained well above 2022 levels of 574,333 students, suggesting the recent decline followed years of rapid growth.

Whether the slowdown proves temporary or marks a lasting shift in students' attitudes toward computer science remains an open question.

"Computer science has historically been a boom-and-bust major; a decline in CS enrollment would not be a new phenomenon for universities," he told Business Insider.

"While I think there's reason to believe CS enrollment may decline in the short-term, many graduates may still find a lot of value in a CS degree," he added.

Light wrote that it will become clearer "as additional cohorts make course and major choices."

sentences.en: Computer science's two-decade boom may have reached a turning point in the age of AI.
sentences.zh: 计算机科学的两个十年的繁荣可能已经到了人工智能时代的转折点。

sentences.en: Bloomberg/Getty Images
sentences.zh: 彭博社/盖蒂图片社

blocks.html_en: The explosive rise of ChatGPT may coincide with a turning point for one of America's fastest-growing college subjects.

sentences.en: The explosive rise of ChatGPT may coincide with a turning point for one of America's fastest-growing college subjects.
sentences.zh: ChatGPT 的爆炸性崛起可能与美国增长最快的大学科目之一的转折点同时发生。

blocks.html_en: Computer science enrollment declined during the 2025-26 academic year for the first time in roughly two decades, according to new research by Jacob Light, an economist at Stanford University's Hoover Institution.

sentences.en: Computer science enrollment declined during the 2025-26 academic year for the first time in roughly two decades, according to new research by Jacob Light, an economist at Stanford University's Hoover Institution.
sentences.zh: 斯坦福大学胡佛研究所经济学家雅各布·莱特 (Jacob Light) 的最新研究显示，2025-26 学年计算机科学入学人数出现大约二十年来的首次下降。

blocks.html_en: While the study doesn't estimate the size of the drop, separate National Student Clearinghouse Research Center data showed undergraduate enrollment in Computer and Information Sciences and Support Services at four-year colleges fell 8.1% year over year in the fall of 2025.

sentences.en: While the study doesn't estimate the size of the drop, separate National Student Clearinghouse Research Center data showed undergraduate enrollment in Computer and Information Sciences and Support Services at four-year colleges fell 8.1% year over year in the fall of 2025.
sentences.zh: 虽然该研究没有估计下降的幅度，但国家学生信息交换中心研究中心的单独数据显示，2025 年秋季四年制大学计算机和信息科学及支持服务专业的本科入学人数同比下降 8.1%。

blocks.html_en: The timing overlaps with the rapid adoption of generative AI, but Light cautioned that the data does not show artificial intelligence caused the decline.

sentences.en: The timing overlaps with the rapid adoption of generative AI, but Light cautioned that the data does not show artificial intelligence caused the decline.
sentences.zh: 这一时机与生成式人工智能的快速采用重叠，但莱特警告说，数据并未显示人工智能导致了衰退。

blocks.html_en: Using a dataset covering more than 50 million course sections offered since 1996 across 1,019 US colleges and universities, Light tracked long-term enrollment patterns alongside the growing capabilities of large language models.

sentences.en: Using a dataset covering more than 50 million course sections offered since 1996 across 1,019 US colleges and universities, Light tracked long-term enrollment patterns alongside the growing capabilities of large language models.
sentences.zh: Light 使用涵盖自 1996 年以来 1,019 所美国学院和大学提供的超过 5000 万个课程部分的数据集，跟踪长期注册模式以及大型语言模型不断增长的功能。

blocks.html_en: Light told Business Insider that, at the average school in his sample, enrollment in computer science classes fell about 4.6% year over year, while computer science's share of overall course enrollment dropped 6.3 percentage points.

sentences.en: Light told Business Insider that, at the average school in his sample, enrollment in computer science classes fell about 4.6% year over year, while computer science's share of overall course enrollment dropped 6.3 percentage points.
sentences.zh: Light 告诉《商业内幕》，在他样本中的平均学校中，计算机科学课程的入学人数同比下降了约 4.6%，而计算机科学占整体课程入学人数的比例下降了 6.3 个百分点。

blocks.html_en: "The most notable recent development is the flattening and reversal in the Computer Science enrollment share trend," Light wrote. "In 2025-26, Computer Science enrollment share declined for the first time in roughly two decades, interrupting a period of sustained and unusually rapid growth."

sentences.en: "The most notable recent development is the flattening and reversal in the Computer Science enrollment share trend," Light wrote.
sentences.zh: “最近最显着的发展是计算机科学招生份额趋势的趋平和逆转，”莱特写道。

sentences.en: "In 2025-26, Computer Science enrollment share declined for the first time in roughly two decades, interrupting a period of sustained and unusually rapid growth."
sentences.zh: “2025-26 年，计算机科学入学率出现大约二十年来的首次下降，中断了一段持续且异常快速的增长时期。”

blocks.html_en: 'Descriptive' rather than 'causal' effect of AI

sentences.en: 'Descriptive' rather than 'causal' effect of AI
sentences.zh: 人工智能的“描述性”效应而非“因果性”效应

blocks.html_en: Computer science had enjoyed years of booming popularity as demand for software engineers and other tech workers surged.

sentences.en: Computer science had enjoyed years of booming popularity as demand for software engineers and other tech workers surged.
sentences.zh: 随着对软件工程师和其他技术人员的需求激增，计算机科学多年来一直蓬勃发展。

blocks.html_en: The number of bachelor's degrees awarded in computer and information sciences more than doubled over the past decade, rising from about 56,000 in 2014 to about 122,000 in 2024, according to data from the National Science Foundation's National Center for Science and Engineering Statistics.

sentences.en: The number of bachelor's degrees awarded in computer and information sciences more than doubled over the past decade, rising from about 56,000 in 2014 to about 122,000 in 2024, according to data from the National Science Foundation's National Center for Science and Engineering Statistics.
sentences.zh: 根据美国国家科学基金会国家科学与工程统计中心的数据，在过去十年中，授予计算机和信息科学学士学位的数量增加了一倍多，从 2014 年的约 56,000 个增加到 2024 年的约 122,000 个。

blocks.html_en: But the arrival of AI assistants capable of writing code, debugging programs, and completing technical assignments has sparked debate about whether the field's value proposition is changing.

sentences.en: But the arrival of AI assistants capable of writing code, debugging programs, and completing technical assignments has sparked debate about whether the field's value proposition is changing.
sentences.zh: 但能够编写代码、调试程序和完成技术任务的人工智能助手的出现引发了关于该领域的价值主张是否正在改变的争论。

blocks.html_en: Light told Business Insider there are five leading explanations for the enrollment drop: a weaker entry-level software engineering job market, uncertainty about the value of computer science degrees in the AI era, weaker academic preparation among incoming students, changes in college demographics linked to immigration policy, and the expansion of competing fields such as data science.

sentences.en: Light told Business Insider there are five leading explanations for the enrollment drop: a weaker entry-level software engineering job market, uncertainty about the value of computer science degrees in the AI era, weaker academic preparation among incoming students, changes in college demographics linked to immigration policy, and the expansion of competing fields such as data science.
sentences.zh: 莱特告诉《商业内幕》，入学率下降有五种主要解释：入门级软件工程就业市场疲软、人工智能时代计算机科学学位价值的不确定性、新生的学术准备较弱、与移民政策相关的大学人口统计变化，以及数据科学等竞争领域的扩张。

blocks.html_en: He added that the data cannot distinguish among those possibilities.

sentences.en: He added that the data cannot distinguish among those possibilities.
sentences.zh: 他补充说，数据无法区分这些可能性。

blocks.html_en: "Therefore, I treat the enrollment patterns as descriptive evidence rather than as an estimate of the causal effect of ChatGPT or AI exposure on student demand," Light wrote.

sentences.en: "Therefore, I treat the enrollment patterns as descriptive evidence rather than as an estimate of the causal effect of ChatGPT or AI exposure on student demand," Light wrote.
sentences.zh: “因此，我将入学模式视为描述性证据，而不是对 ChatGPT 或人工智能暴露对学生需求的因果影响的估计，”Light 写道。

blocks.html_en: The finding is also consistent with data from the National Student Clearinghouse Research Center.

sentences.en: The finding is also consistent with data from the National Student Clearinghouse Research Center.
sentences.zh: 这一发现也与国家学生信息交换所研究中心的数据一致。

blocks.html_en: Its Fall 2025 enrollment report found that undergraduate enrollment in Computer and Information Sciences and Support Services at four-year colleges fell 8.1% year over year, from about 659,700 students in 2024 to 606,100 in 2025.

sentences.en: Its Fall 2025 enrollment report found that undergraduate enrollment in Computer and Information Sciences and Support Services at four-year colleges fell 8.1% year over year, from about 659,700 students in 2024 to 606,100 in 2025.
sentences.zh: 其2025年秋季入学报告发现，四年制大学计算机与信息科学及支持服务本科生入学人数同比下降8.1%，从2024年的约659,700名学生减少到2025年的606,100名学生。

blocks.html_en: Even so, enrollment remained well above 2022 levels of 574,333 students, suggesting the recent decline followed years of rapid growth.

sentences.en: Even so, enrollment remained well above 2022 levels of 574,333 students, suggesting the recent decline followed years of rapid growth.
sentences.zh: 即便如此，入学人数仍远高于 2022 年 574,333 名学生的水平，这表明在经历了多年的快速增长之后，近期出现了下降。

blocks.html_en: Whether the slowdown proves temporary or marks a lasting shift in students' attitudes toward computer science remains an open question.

sentences.en: Whether the slowdown proves temporary or marks a lasting shift in students' attitudes toward computer science remains an open question.
sentences.zh: 这种放缓是暂时的还是标志着学生对计算机科学态度的持久转变仍然是一个悬而未决的问题。

blocks.html_en: "Computer science has historically been a boom-and-bust major; a decline in CS enrollment would not be a new phenomenon for universities," he told Business Insider.

sentences.en: "Computer science has historically been a boom-and-bust major; a decline in CS enrollment would not be a new phenomenon for universities," he told Business Insider.
sentences.zh: 他对《商业内幕》表示：“计算机科学从历史上看一直是一个繁荣与萧条的专业；计算机科学入学人数的下降对于大学来说并不是什么新现象。”

blocks.html_en: "While I think there's reason to believe CS enrollment may decline in the short-term, many graduates may still find a lot of value in a CS degree," he added.

sentences.en: "While I think there's reason to believe CS enrollment may decline in the short-term, many graduates may still find a lot of value in a CS degree," he added.
sentences.zh: “虽然我认为有理由相信计算机科学的入学人数可能会在短期内下降，但许多毕业生可能仍然会发现计算机科学学位有很大的价值，”他补充道。

blocks.html_en: Light wrote that it will become clearer "as additional cohorts make course and major choices."

sentences.en: Light wrote that it will become clearer "as additional cohorts make course and major choices."
sentences.zh: 莱特写道，“随着更多的人做出课程和主要选择”，这一点将会变得更加清晰。

content_html_zh:
作者：Thibault Spirlet 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。

ChatGPT 的爆炸性崛起可能与美国增长最快的大学科目之一的转折点同时发生。

斯坦福大学胡佛研究所经济学家雅各布·莱特 (Jacob Light) 的最新研究显示，2025-26 学年计算机科学入学人数出现大约二十年来的首次下降。

虽然该研究没有估计下降的幅度，但国家学生信息交换中心研究中心的单独数据显示，2025 年秋季四年制大学计算机和信息科学及支持服务专业的本科入学人数同比下降 8.1%。

这一时机与生成式人工智能的快速采用重叠，但莱特警告说，数据并未显示人工智能导致了衰退。

Light 使用涵盖自 1996 年以来 1,019 所美国学院和大学提供的超过 5000 万个课程部分的数据集，跟踪长期注册模式以及大型语言模型不断增长的功能。

Light 告诉《商业内幕》，在他样本中的平均学校中，计算机科学课程的入学人数同比下降了约 4.6%，而计算机科学占整体课程入学人数的比例下降了 6.3 个百分点。

“最近最显着的发展是计算机科学招生份额趋势的趋平和逆转，”莱特写道。 “2025-26 年，计算机科学入学率出现大约二十年来的首次下降，中断了一段持续且异常快速的增长时期。”

人工智能的“描述性”效应而非“因果性”效应

随着对软件工程师和其他技术人员的需求激增，计算机科学多年来一直蓬勃发展。

根据美国国家科学基金会国家科学与工程统计中心的数据，在过去十年中，授予计算机和信息科学学士学位的数量增加了一倍多，从 2014 年的约 56,000 个增加到 2024 年的约 122,000 个。

但能够编写代码、调试程序和完成技术任务的人工智能助手的出现引发了关于该领域的价值主张是否正在改变的争论。

莱特告诉《商业内幕》，入学率下降有五种主要解释：入门级软件工程就业市场疲软、人工智能时代计算机科学学位价值的不确定性、新生的学术准备较弱、与移民政策相关的大学人口统计变化，以及数据科学等竞争领域的扩张。

他补充说，数据无法区分这些可能性。

“因此，我将入学模式视为描述性证据，而不是对 ChatGPT 或人工智能暴露对学生需求的因果影响的估计，”Light 写道。

这一发现也与国家学生信息交换所研究中心的数据一致。

其2025年秋季入学报告发现，四年制大学计算机与信息科学及支持服务本科生入学人数同比下降8.1%，从2024年的约659,700名学生减少到2025年的606,100名学生。

即便如此，入学人数仍远高于 2022 年 574,333 名学生的水平，这表明在经历了多年的快速增长之后，近期出现了下降。

这种放缓是暂时的还是标志着学生对计算机科学态度的持久转变仍然是一个悬而未决的问题。

他对《商业内幕》表示：“计算机科学从历史上看一直是一个繁荣与萧条的专业；计算机科学入学人数的下降对于大学来说并不是什么新现象。”

“虽然我认为有理由相信计算机科学的入学人数可能会在短期内下降，但许多毕业生可能仍然会发现计算机科学学位有很大的价值，”他补充道。

莱特写道，“随着更多的人做出课程和主要选择”，这一点将会变得更加清晰。

蒂博是《商业内幕》伦敦办事处的商业记者。他报道财富、工作和技术的交叉点——重点关注全球经济、人工智能对工作场所、工作和认知技能的影响，以及经济变化如何影响职业。 在加入趋势团队之前，蒂博在新闻台报道国际事务，包括俄罗斯-乌克兰战争、南海紧张局势以及俄罗斯经济。他此前曾在《每日快报》工作，并在法新社、Politico Europe 和 Factal.Il parle français 实习。 请发送电子邮件至 tspirlet@businessinsider.com，通过 LinkedIn @ThibaultSpirlet 与他联系，或在 X @ThibaultSpirlet 和 BlueSky @thibaultspirlet.bsky.social.Expertise 上关注他
